query: Nvidia N1 N1X laptop OEM Dell Lenovo availability pre-order purchase date June 2026
answer:
I have comprehensive findings from the first research task. Here is my full synthesis:

---

# Nvidia N1 / N1X (RTX Spark) Laptop Availability — Research Report

## Overview

The Nvidia "N1" and "N1X" are internal chip designations for what Nvidia officially branded as the **NVIDIA RTX Spark** family. These are ARM-based laptop/desktop processors combining Nvidia's Grace CPU and Blackwell RTX GPU architectures on a single chip, manufactured at TSMC's 3nm node in partnership with MediaTek. They were **announced on June 1, 2026** at Nvidia's GTC Taipei keynote during Computex 2026.

## Key Specifications

- **CPU:** Custom 20-core Grace ARM CPU (built with MediaTek, connected via NVLink)
- **GPU:** Blackwell RTX with 6,144 CUDA cores (~RTX 5070 Laptop equivalent)
- **AI Performance:** 1 petaflop
- **Memory:** Up to 128GB unified LPDDR5X (lower tiers down to 16GB)
- **Process:** TSMC 3nm
- **Power:** Scales from low single-digit watts up to 80W

## Confirmed OEM Laptops (8 at launch)

| OEM | Model |
|-----|-------|
| **Dell** | **XPS 16** |
| **Lenovo** | **Yoga Pro 9n** |
| **Microsoft** | Surface Laptop Ultra |
| **Asus** | ProArt P14 and ProArt P16 |
| **HP** | OmniBook X 14 and OmniBook Ultra 16 |
| **MSI** | Prestige N16 Flip AI Plus |

Additional partners Acer and Gigabyte have unnamed models in development. Nvidia says **30+ laptops and 10+ desktops** are in the pipeline.

## Availability & Pre-Order Status (as of June 2, 2026)

- **Announcement date:** June 1, 2026
- **Expected launch:** **Fall 2026** (no specific month)
- **Pre-orders:** **NOT yet available** — no pre-order dates have been announced by any OEM
- **Current status:** Just announced; zero benchmarks or comparative performance data shared
- Nvidia says more details (benchmarks, pricing, exact dates) will come "closer to launch"

## Pricing

- **No official prices announced** from any OEM
- Nvidia described the initial launch as targeting **"premium price points"**
- Analysts and press estimate the range at **$2,000–$2,500+** for initial models
- Comparable reference points:
  - DGX Spark desktop (same GB10 silicon): ~$4,700
  - AMD Strix Halo 128GB laptops: $3,000–$3,300
- Lower-cost RTX Spark variants (as little as 16GB RAM) are planned for later

## Software Ecosystem Readiness

- Adobe rearchitecting Photoshop/Premiere for RTX Spark (claiming 2x AI/graphics speedup)
- Microsoft preparing Windows security primitives for AI agents
- Games with anti-cheat (Valorant, LoL, PUBG, Fortnite) adding Windows on ARM support
- Full CUDA-X software stack claimed to run on RTX Spark
- No Linux driver support confirmed

## Key Unknowns

- Exact launch dates and pre-order windows for Dell XPS 16 and Lenovo Yoga Pro 9n
- Specific pricing from any OEM
- Performance benchmarks vs. Intel, AMD, Apple, or Qualcomm
- Manufacturing location (Nvidia declined to comment)
- Detailed specs (screen, battery, storage) for individual OEM models

---

**Bottom line for June 2026:** The Dell XPS 16 and Lenovo Yoga Pro 9n with Nvidia RTX Spark (N1/N1X) chips have been **announced but are not available for purchase or pre-order**. The expected availability window is **fall 2026**, with no specific dates or pricing disclosed yet.

---

key_facts: Nvidia N1/N1X are internal chip designations for the officially branded 'NVIDIA RTX Spark' family of ARM-based processors, announced June 1, 2026 at GTC Taipei / Computex 2026.
RTX Spark combines a 20-core Grace ARM CPU with a Blackwell RTX GPU (6,144 CUDA cores) on a single TSMC 3nm chip, built in partnership with MediaTek.
The chip delivers 1 petaflop of AI performance with up to 128GB unified LPDDR5X memory, scaling from low single-digit watts to 80W.
Dell XPS 16 and Lenovo Yoga Pro 9n are among 8 confirmed launch laptops with RTX Spark chips.
Other confirmed OEM laptops: Microsoft Surface Laptop Ultra, Asus ProArt P14/P16, HP OmniBook X 14 / OmniBook Ultra 16, MSI Prestige N16 Flip AI Plus.
Over 30 laptops and 10 desktops with RTX Spark are in development across OEMs including Acer and Gigabyte.
As of June 2, 2026, NO pre-orders are available from any OEM — the products were just announced on June 1, 2026.
Expected launch window is Fall 2026, with no specific month or date disclosed.
No official pricing has been announced; Nvidia describes initial launch as targeting 'premium price points' — analysts estimate $2,000–$2,500+.
Lower-cost RTX Spark variants with as little as 16GB RAM are planned for after the initial premium launch.
Nvidia has shared zero performance benchmarks vs. Intel, AMD, Apple, or Qualcomm — details promised 'closer to launch'.
GPU performance is roughly equivalent to an RTX 5070 Laptop GPU.
Adobe is rearchitecting Photoshop and Premiere for RTX Spark; full CUDA-X stack is claimed to run on the platform.
No Linux driver support has been confirmed; focus is on Windows only.
